short: An optimizer that orthogonalizes weight updates for more uniform learning.
definition: Muon is an optimizer (the part of training that adjusts the model's internal numbers) that smooths each adjustment, then reshapes it so the model learns at a more even pace across all of a layer's numbers rather than over-correcting in a few directions. It has reached a good solution faster than the popular AdamW optimizer on some language-model training runs, while using a similar amount of memory.
